meta.name: VoiceAttack Search Index
meta.description: Comprehensive reference covering tokens, actions, settings, speech recognition, profiles, hotkeys, troubleshooting, and more.

entries.title: Installation Requirements
entries.description: VoiceAttack requires Windows 7 or later, .NET Framework 4.7.2 or higher (included in Windows 10/11). Also requires a microphone for speech recognition.

entries.title: Installation Steps
entries.description: Download the installer from voiceattack.com, run it, follow the prompts. After installation, launch VoiceAttack and complete the speech recognition training via the Windows Speech Recognition control panel for best accuracy.

entries.title: Windows Speech Recognizer (Built‑in)
entries.description: The default recognizer on Windows. Requires training to your voice for best results. Works with most languages. Enable via VoiceAttack options or Windows Speech Recognition settings.

entries.title: Microsoft Speech Platform (SP 11)
entries.description: Alternative recognizer. Optimised for noisy environments, no training required. Must be installed separately: SpeechPlatformRuntime.msi + language pack (MSSpeech_SR_en-US_TELE.msi). Can be selected in VoiceAttack Options.

entries.title: Speech Recognition Confidence
entries.description: VoiceAttack uses a confidence score (0–100) for each recognised phrase. You can set a per‑command minimum confidence level. Use {CMDCONFIDENCE} to see the score. Low-confidence commands can be rejected or executed with a warning.

entries.title: Dictation Mode
entries.description: Allows free‑form speech input (not just commands). Can be used for chat, note‑taking, or filling text fields. Use {DICTATION} tokens to access the dictation buffer. Toggle via commands or hotkey.

entries.title: Listening Modes
entries.description: Two main modes: 'Always Listening' (continuous) or 'Push‑to‑Listen' (hold a key/button). Also supports a 'Listening Override' keyword (e.g., 'Computer') to wake the system.

entries.title: Command Trigger: 'When I Say'
entries.description: The primary way to trigger a command — define a spoken phrase (or multiple, separated by semicolons). Supports wildcards (*) and dynamic segments. Use {CMDSEGMENT} to capture parts of the phrase.

entries.title: Command Trigger: 'When a Key is Pressed'
entries.description: Trigger a command via keyboard shortcut. Supports single keys, combos (Ctrl+Shift+A), and sequences. Also supports double‑tap and long‑press detection.

entries.title: Command Trigger: 'When a Mouse Button is Pressed'
entries.description: Trigger via mouse buttons (left, right, middle, forward, back). Supports double‑tap and long‑press. Can also trigger on mouse wheel movement or axis changes.

entries.title: Command Trigger: 'When a Joystick Button is Pressed'
entries.description: Trigger via joystick/gamepad buttons (up to 128 buttons per device). Supports double‑tap and long‑press. Also supports POV hat changes and axis thresholds.

entries.title: Command Trigger: 'When a Plugin Command Fires'
entries.description: Commands can be triggered by external plugins (C#) sending events to VoiceAttack. Useful for integration with games, simulators, or other applications.

entries.title: Action: 'Speak Text' (TTS)
entries.description: Speaks a text string using the selected Windows TTS voice. Supports tokens and variables. Can also speak with specific rate, volume, and pitch. Use 'Wait until speaking completes' to sequence actions.

entries.title: Action: 'Play a Sound'
entries.description: Plays a WAV, MP3, or other audio file. Supports relative paths (Sounds folder). Can loop, adjust volume, and wait for completion. {STATE_AUDIOCOUNT} and {STATE_AUDIOPOS} provide state feedback.

entries.title: Action: 'Press a Key'
entries.description: Simulates keyboard input. Can press single keys, combos (Ctrl+C), or hold/release with variable durations. Also supports sending text strings with {TXT} variables.

entries.title: Action: 'Move Mouse'
entries.description: Moves the mouse cursor to absolute coordinates (screen or window‑relative) or relative offsets. Can also move to a window's center or specific UI element coordinates.

entries.title: Action: 'Click Mouse'
entries.description: Simulates mouse clicks (left, right, middle, forward, back). Supports single, double, and triple clicks. Can also perform drag‑and‑drop sequences.

entries.title: Action: 'Scroll Mouse Wheel'
entries.description: Simulates mouse wheel scrolling (up/down) with adjustable step count. Useful for scrolling documents, web pages, or in‑game menus.

entries.title: Action: 'Set a Variable'
entries.description: Sets the value of a variable. Supports text (TXT), integer (INT), decimal (DEC), boolean (BOOL), date, and small integer (SMALL) types. Can set to static values, tokens, or results of expressions.

entries.title: Action: 'If / Else If / Else' (Conditions)
entries.description: Conditional branching. Supports comparisons (==, !=, <, >, <=, >=), logical operators (AND, OR, NOT), string matching (LIKE, CONTAINS), and evaluation of {EXP} expressions. Essential for dynamic command logic.

entries.title: Action: 'Loop' (While / For / Repeat)
entries.description: Repeats a block of actions. Supports 'Repeat X times', 'While condition is true', and 'For each item in collection'. Use {LOOPINDEX} to track iteration number. Break out with 'Exit Loop' action.

entries.title: Action: 'Execute a Command'
entries.description: Calls another command (in the same or another profile). Supports passing parameters via variables. Can wait for completion or run asynchronously. Use {CMDACTIVE} to check if a command is already running.

entries.title: Action: 'Exit Command'
entries.description: Immediately stops execution of the current command. Can be placed anywhere in the action list. Useful for early termination based on conditions.

entries.title: Action: 'Pause' (Delay)
entries.description: Pauses command execution for a specified duration (in milliseconds or seconds). Useful for timing sequences or waiting for UI changes.

entries.title: Action: 'Set Clipboard Text'
entries.description: Copies text to the Windows clipboard. Can use tokens and variables. Use {CLIP} to read the current clipboard content.

entries.title: Action: 'Control Audio Device'
entries.description: Adjusts system volume, mute status, or sets the default playback/recording device. Can target specific applications via the System Volume Mixer.

entries.title: Action: 'Control Window'
entries.description: Manipulates windows: minimise, maximise, restore, close, move, resize, set focus, or send text to a specific window. Can target by title or process name.

entries.title: Action: 'File Operations'
entries.description: Create, delete, copy, move, or rename files and folders. Can also read/write text files, parse CSV, or check file/directory existence.

entries.title: Action: 'Run a Plugin'
entries.description: Calls a method in a loaded C# plugin. Allows extending VoiceAttack with custom functionality (e.g., game integrations, hardware control, web APIs).

entries.title: Variable Types
entries.description: Text (TXT) – any string. Integer (INT) – whole numbers. Decimal (DEC) – fractional numbers. Boolean (BOOL) – True/False. Small Integer (SMALL) – 0–9999 for conditions. Date/Time – stores timestamps. All are persisted per profile unless cleared.

entries.title: Variable Scope
entries.description: Variables are profile‑local by default. They persist while the profile is loaded. Use 'Set a Variable' with the 'Global' option to share across profiles. Also supports command‑local variables (temporary) via 'Set Local Variable'.

entries.title: Expression Evaluation {EXP}
entries.description: Evaluates arithmetic, text, or logical expressions. Supports +, -, *, /, %, parentheses, comparisons, AND/OR/NOT, LIKE, TRIM, SUBSTRING, LEN, IIF (inline if). Use {EXPDECINV} for invariant culture (point as decimal separator).

entries.title: Text Utility Tokens
entries.description: Includes {TXT LEN}, {TXT UPPER}, {TXT LOWER}, {TXT TRIM}, {TXT REPLACEVAR}, {TXT REGEXREPLACE}, {TXTSUBSTR}, {TXTPOS}, {TXTOCcURRENCES}. Also {TXT RANDOM} for random selection, {TXTWORDTONUM} for word‑to‑number conversion.

entries.title: Profile Loading & Switching
entries.description: Profiles contain all commands, variables, and settings. Load a profile via the 'Load Profile' action, the toolbar, or command-line arguments. Use {PROFILE} to get the current profile name, {PREVIOUSPROFILE} for the last one.

entries.title: Profile Author Tags
entries.description: Each profile can store three author tags (text fields). Useful for versioning, authorship, or metadata. Access via {PROFILE_AT1}, {PROFILE_AT2}, {PROFILE_AT3} and their history variants.

entries.title: Global Hotkeys
entries.description: VoiceAttack can register global hotkeys that work even when the app is not in focus. Set in Options → Hotkeys. Can be used to toggle listening, mute, or trigger specific commands.

entries.title: Command Queues
entries.description: Allows commands to be executed in sequence, one after another, even if they are triggered simultaneously. Can pause, resume, or check queue status. Useful for complex automation workflows.

entries.title: Plugin SDK (C#)
entries.description: VoiceAttack exposes a .NET SDK for writing plugins. Plugins can add custom actions, triggers, or interact with external systems. Place compiled DLLs in the 'Apps' folder.

entries.title: Audio Output Types
entries.description: Three options: Legacy (WaveOut), Windows (WASAPI), Integrated (uses the same device as Windows sounds). Select in Options → Audio. {STATE_AUDIOOUTPUTTYPE} reports the current setting.

entries.title: TTS Voice Selection
entries.description: Select from any installed Windows TTS voices. Adjust rate, volume, and pitch per command. Use 'Speak Text' with tokens to create dynamic spoken messages.

entries.title: System State Tokens
entries.description: Extensive set of tokens to query system state: {STATE_KEYSTATE}, {STATE_ANYKEYDOWN}, {STATE_LEFTMOUSEBUTTON}, {STATE_CPU}, {STATE_RAMAVAILABLE}, {STATE_SYSVOL}, {STATE_SYSMUTE}, {STATE_ENV}, {STATE_CULTURE}, and dozens more.

entries.title: Window State Tokens
entries.description: Tokens like {ACTIVEWINDOWTITLE}, {ACTIVEWINDOWPROCESSNAME}, {WINDOWEXISTS}, {WINDOWFOREGROUND}, {WINDOWCOUNT}, {WINDOWTITLEUNDERMOUSE}. Essential for automation with specific applications.

entries.title: Joystick State Tokens
entries.description: Full access to joystick/gamepad states: axis values (X, Y, Z, rotation, sliders), button states, POV hat directions. Supports up to 4 sticks. Tokens like {STATE_JOYSTICK1X}, {STATE_JOYSTICK2BUTTON}, etc.

entries.title: Command Information Tokens
entries.description: Tokens providing runtime command data: {CMD} (command name), {CMDSEGMENT} (dynamic segment), {CMDACTION} (trigger method), {CMDCONFIDENCE} (confidence score), {CMD_BEFORE}/{CMD_AFTER} (wildcard captures), {ISCOMPOSITE}, {PREFIX}/{SUFFIX}.

entries.title: History Tokens
entries.description: Access command history: {LASTSPOKENCMD}, {PREVIOUSSPOKENCMD}, {SPOKENCMD:value}, {LASTSPOKEN}, {PREVIOUSSPOKEN}. Also profile history: {PROFILE:value}, {PREVIOUSPROFILE}, {NEXTPROFILE}.

entries.title: Miscellaneous Utility Tokens
entries.description: {RANDOM} – random number. {GUID} – new unique ID. {ASCII} – character by code. {SPACE}, {NEWLINE}, {TAB}, {DOUBLEQUOTE}. {CLIP} – clipboard content. {DICTATION} – dictation buffer.

entries.title: Troubleshooting: Log Files
entries.description: VoiceAttack writes logs to the 'Logs' folder (inside installation directory). Also shows a live console. Use {STATE_VA_LOGREVERSED} to check log orientation. Enable detailed logging in Options → Troubleshooting.

entries.title: Troubleshooting: Reset Configuration
entries.description: If VoiceAttack behaves unexpectedly, delete the settings file (VoiceAttack.dat) in the installation folder. Or use 'Restore Defaults' in Options. Backup your profiles first.

entries.title: Troubleshooting: Speech Recognition Issues
entries.description: Check microphone selection, run Windows Speech Recognition training, increase confidence thresholds, try the Microsoft Speech Platform recogniser, or adjust the 'Noise Suppression' setting in Options.

entries.title: Composite (Prefix+Suffix) Commands
entries.description: A special command structure where a single command can have a prefix and suffix. Example: 'set volume to 5' where 'set volume to' is prefix and '5' is suffix. Supports wildcards and dynamic segments.

entries.title: Command Categories
entries.description: Organise commands into categories (e.g., 'Media', 'Games', 'System'). Useful for filtering and organising large profiles. Access category info via {CATEGORY}, {PREFIX_CATEGORY}, {SUFFIX_CATEGORY}.

entries.title: Skip If Condition (Inline)
entries.description: Each action can have a 'Skip If' condition. If the condition evaluates to true, that action is skipped without aborting the command. Useful for compact conditional logic.

entries.title: Double‑Tap & Long‑Press Detection
entries.description: For key, mouse, or joystick triggers, you can specify a double‑tap interval and a long‑press duration. Use {CMDDOUBLETAPINVOKED} and {CMDLONGPRESSINVOKED} to detect which one fired.

entries.title: Wildcards in Phrases
entries.description: Use * (asterisk) as a wildcard in 'When I Say' phrases. Example: 'open *' captures 'open browser', 'open notepad', etc. Use {CMD_BEFORE}, {CMD_AFTER}, or {CMD_WILDCARDKEY} to access the captured text.

entries.title: Dynamic Segments {CMDSEGMENT}
entries.description: For commands with multiple parts, use {CMDSEGMENT:0}, {CMDSEGMENT:1}, etc., to capture each word or phrase. Example: 'set volume to [0..100]' – segment 0 is 'set', segment 1 is 'volume', etc.

entries.title: Loop Controls (Break, Continue)
entries.description: Inside a loop, use 'Exit Loop' to break out early, or 'Continue Loop' to skip the rest of the current iteration and move to the next.

entries.title: Action: 'Comment'
entries.description: Adds a comment or note to the command action list. Does nothing during execution but helps document complex command logic.

entries.title: Action: 'Inline Function' (Execute C# Code)
entries.description: Allows executing a small snippet of C# code directly within an action. Useful for complex calculations or operations not covered by built-in actions.

entries.title: Options / Settings Overview
entries.description: Access via the gear icon. Main tabs: General (listening, hotkeys), Speech (recogniser, confidence, noise), Audio (devices, TTS), Joystick (enable/disable, axes), Plugins, Troubleshooting (logging, performance).

entries.title: Command‑Line Arguments
entries.description: Launch VoiceAttack with arguments: /profile 'name' to load a profile, /command 'name' to execute a command on startup, /listen to start listening, /minimized to start hidden. Useful for game launchers.

entries.title: Global Keyboard Shortcuts (Options)
entries.description: Set global shortcuts in Options → Hotkeys. Examples: toggle listening, mute microphone, reload profile, open command editor. These work even when VoiceAttack is in the background.

entries.title: Profile Export / Import
entries.description: Export profiles as .vap files (XML) for backup or sharing. Import via File → Import Profile. Profiles can include commands, variables, and settings. Also supports merging profiles.

entries.title: Community & Resources
entries.description: Official forum: voiceattack.com/forum. Community profiles and plugins available on the forum and Discord. Also many YouTube tutorials and third‑party integrations (e.g., Elite Dangerous, Star Citizen).
